summary.what: Make .timbersignore discoverable (pending hint, --explain, help topic, onboard) and add a doctor lint for the author:dependabot[bot] glob footgun
summary.why: An agent had to research whether author-matching existed and could silently mis-write 'author:dependabot[bot]' (a no-op glob) — surfacing the exemption lever and guarding the footgun turns a research-and-trap into a one-liner
summary.how: doctor lint flags literal-looking [..] classes in author:/msg: globs; pending prints a .timbersignore hint when non-empty; 'pending --explain' classifies every commit via a new shared pendingRange() (extracted from getPendingCommits, behavior-preserving); 'help timbersignore' topic + onboard blurb document the three rule types and canonical bot recipe

notes: Refactored the delicate pending gate: extracted pendingRange() (raw range + docSet/ackedSet) so the filtering gate and the new classifier share one range-resolution path instead of duplicating the side-branch short-circuits — the 40K-line gate test suite is the regression net and stayed green. Bundled four beads into one commit because they're one cohesive .timbersignore-UX theme and pending.go carries changes for two of them (clean split would need git-add-p). Verified all four surfaces live, including the doctor warn path on a temp bad glob.
